# card-html-rules.md â€” Use Case Card HTML Specification

The card is **one screen inside the combined file** â€” it is not a standalone HTML file.
Read `assets/three-use-case-card.html` as the template reference, but the output lives inside
`[client-slug]-use-case.html` as `<div id="screen-card" class="screen active">`.

Do not rebuild the SVG laptop frame or phone notification SVG from scratch â€” copy them verbatim
from the template.

---

## Combined file structure

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<style>
  /* 1. Shared styles */
  :root { --brand: ...; --brand-dark: ...; --brand-light: ...; --brand-mid: ...; }
  .screen { display: none; width: 100vw; height: 100vh; overflow: hidden; }
  .screen.active { display: flex; }

  /* 2. Card-specific styles â€” scope everything under #screen-card */
  #screen-card .card { ... }
  #screen-card .card-header { ... }
  /* etc. */

  /* 3. Journey-specific styles â€” scope everything under #screen-journey */
  #screen-journey .journey-container { ... }
  /* etc. */
</style>
</head>
<body>
  <div id="screen-card" class="screen active">
    <!-- full card HTML -->
  </div>
  <div id="screen-journey" class="screen">
    <!-- full journey HTML -->
  </div>
  <script>
    function showScreen(id) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.screen').forEach(s => s.classList.remove('active'));
      document.getElementById(id).classList.add('active');
      if (id === 'screen-journey') { journeyReset(); journeyAutoAnimate(); }
    }
    /* card device-panel animation */
    /* journey animation engine â€” journeyReset() and journeyAutoAnimate() */
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

**CSS scoping rule:** Every card CSS selector must be prefixed with `#screen-card` and every
journey CSS selector must be prefixed with `#screen-journey`. This prevents rule collisions
between the two screens. Shared rules (`:root` variables, `.screen`) live at the root level.

---

## Card screen structure

```
#screen-card
  .card
    .card-header          â† client logo + type badge + Adobe wordmark
    .sub-header           â† technology pill strip
    .card-body (58% / 42% grid)
      .panel-left         â† use case statement + 2Ã—2 value grid
      .panel-right        â† laptop with video (slide 1) + phone with notification (slide 2)
    .card-footer          â† 3 metric stats on dark brand background
```

---

## CSS brand colour variables

```css
:root {
  --brand:       #HEXCODE;   /* primary â€” eyebrow, pills, left accent bar, right panel bg */
  --brand-dark:  #HEXCODE;   /* footer background */
  --brand-light: #HEXCODE;   /* pill/badge backgrounds, value-grid icon bg */
  --brand-mid:   #HEXCODE;   /* pill/badge borders, value-grid icon border */
}
```

---

## Header

```html
<div class="card-header">
  <div class="header-logo">
    <img src="https://logo.clearbit.com/[clientdomain]" alt="[Client Name]"
         onerror="this.style.display='none'; document.getElementById('logo-fb').style.display='block'">
    <span class="logo-fallback" id="logo-fb" style="display:none">[Client Name]</span>
  </div>
  <div></div>
  <div class="header-right">
    <span class="type-badge">Upsell</span>  <!-- Retention / Upsell / Cross-sell / Acquisition -->
    <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Adobe" style="height:22px; width:auto; display:block;">
  </div>
</div>
```

The type badge uses `--brand-light` background and `--brand` text â€” it updates automatically via CSS variables.

---

## Sub-header (technology strip)

```html
<div class="sub-header">
  <span class="sub-header-label">Technology</span>
  <span class="tech-pill">Real-Time CDP</span>
  <span class="tech-pill">Journey Optimizer</span>
  <!-- Add/remove tech-pill elements to match the selected use case products -->
</div>
```

---

## Left panel

### Accent bar
The brand-coloured vertical bar on the left edge is a CSS `::before` pseudo-element â€” it updates automatically when `--brand` is set. Do not add it as an HTML element.

### Use case statement
```html
<div class="uc-statement">
  I want to [action] <span class="client-name">[Client Name]</span> customers
  [rest of statement]
</div>
```

Wrap the client name in `<span class="client-name">` â€” this applies `color: var(--brand)`. The opening and closing quote marks are added automatically by CSS (`::before`/`::after`).

### Value grid (2Ã—2)
Four outcomes from the use case bullets. Each item needs a simple icon character and a short label (max 6 words):

```html
<div class="value-grid">
  <div class="value-grid-item">
    <div class="value-grid-icon">â†—</div>
    <span class="value-grid-text">Short outcome label</span>
  </div>
  <!-- repeat Ã— 4 -->
</div>
```

Recommended icon characters: `â†— âš¡ â—Ž â†’ âœ¦ â¬¡ âŠ• â—ˆ`

---

## Right panel â€” two-slide device animation

The right panel auto-cycles through two states:

- **State 1 (default):** Laptop centred in the panel, phone hidden
- **State 2 (after 5 seconds):** Laptop shifts top-left, phone fades in bottom-right

This is triggered by adding `.active` to `#devicePanel` via a `setTimeout(activate, 5000)` in the script. Copy this script verbatim from the template.

### Laptop

The laptop SVG frame and mask are **fixed â€” copy verbatim**. Only the video source changes.

**The laptop frame must use `viewBox=”0 0 300 220”` with the full aluminium structure.** Never replace it with simplified dark rectangles or a different viewBox (e.g. `viewBox=”0 0 750 480”`). A simplified frame renders as a plain black box — no aluminium, no bezel, no hinge. The required elements are:
- Gradients: `lgLidAl`, `lgLidSheen`, `lgHingeCyl`
- `lid-mask` — cuts the screen opening from the aluminium lid so the video shows through
- Trackpad, hinge bar, webcam notch

If ANY of these are missing, the laptop will look like a dark rectangle with no frame.

#### Video selection â€” read this carefully

| Filename | Show when |
|----------|-----------|
| `AJO-Video.mp4` | Journey Optimizer is the **hero product** â€” use case is primarily about journey orchestration, personalised messaging, triggered campaigns, or abandoned journey recovery |
| `RT-CDP-Video.mp4` | Real-Time CDP is the **hero product** â€” use case is primarily about audience segmentation, profile unification, real-time data activation, or identity resolution |
| `CJA.mp4` | Customer Journey Analytics is the **hero product** â€” use case is primarily about attribution, cross-channel reporting, or customer journey measurement |

**Decision rule:** Identify the single product that delivers the primary outcome. If multiple products are involved, use the one that does the most distinctive work in this use case.

Examples:
- "Identify customers approaching contract end and deliver personalised retention offers" â†’ **AJO-Video** (orchestration + messaging is the hero)
- "Build a real-time segment of high-intent visitors and activate to paid media" â†’ **RT-CDP-Video** (segmentation + activation is the hero)
- "Measure the full customer journey from acquisition to conversion" â†’ **CJA** (analytics is the hero)

#### Video embedding â€” base64 data URI

The video must be **embedded as a base64 data URI** so the HTML file is fully self-contained.
Do not use a relative file path (`src="AJO-Video.mp4"`) â€” this will break whenever the file is
opened from a different location.

**Process:**

1. Use PowerShell to read the correct video and encode it:
```powershell
$b64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\Users\dennison\.claude\skills\user\aep-demo-use-case\assets\AJO-Video.mp4'))
```

2. Write the data URI into the HTML:
```html
<div class="laptop-screen">
  <video autoplay loop muted playsinline>
    <source src="data:video/mp4;base64,[BASE64STRING]" type="video/mp4">
  </video>
</div>
```

Replace `[BASE64STRING]` with the full base64 output from step 1.

**Screen position** (percentages of laptop wrapper, do not change):
```css
#screen-card .laptop-screen {
  left: 2.67%;
  top: 2.73%;
  width: 94.67%;
  height: 67.27%;
}
```

### Phone notification SVG

Copy this block verbatim into `.panel-right`. Update only the five marked elements.

```html
<div class=”phone-wrapper”>
  <svg viewBox=”0 0 220 455” xmlns=”http://www.w3.org/2000/svg”>
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id=”phoneBody” x1=”0” y1=”0” x2=”1” y2=”0”>
        <stop offset=”0%”   stop-color=”#0E0E10”/>
        <stop offset=”20%”  stop-color=”#1C1C1E”/>
        <stop offset=”80%”  stop-color=”#1C1C1E”/>
        <stop offset=”100%” stop-color=”#0E0E10”/>
      </linearGradient>
      <linearGradient id=”sideFrame” x1=”0” y1=”0” x2=”1” y2=”0”>
        <stop offset=”0%”   stop-color=”#2A2A2C”/>
        <stop offset=”25%”  stop-color=”#6C6C70”/>
        <stop offset=”50%”  stop-color=”#9A9A9E”/>
        <stop offset=”75%”  stop-color=”#6C6C70”/>
        <stop offset=”100%” stop-color=”#2A2A2C”/>
      </linearGradient>
      <linearGradient id=”wallpaper” x1=”0.2” y1=”0” x2=”0.8” y2=”1”>
        <stop offset=”0%”   stop-color=”#1e4899”/>
        <stop offset=”50%”  stop-color=”#163580”/>
        <stop offset=”100%” stop-color=”#0a1a45”/>
      </linearGradient>
      <clipPath id=”screenClip”>
        <rect x=”9” y=”9” width=”202” height=”437” rx=”39”/>
      </clipPath>
      <filter id=”cardShadow” x=”-8%” y=”-8%” width=”116%” height=”120%”>
        <feDropShadow dx=”0” dy=”2” stdDeviation=”4” flood-color=”#000” flood-opacity=”0.10”/>
      </filter>
    </defs>
    <rect x=”2” y=”2” width=”216” height=”451” rx=”46” fill=”url(#phoneBody)”/>
    <rect x=”2” y=”2” width=”216” height=”451” rx=”46” fill=”none” stroke=”url(#sideFrame)” stroke-width=”3.5”/>
    <rect x=”9” y=”9” width=”202” height=”437” rx=”39” fill=”white”/>
    <g clip-path=”url(#screenClip)”>
      <rect x=”9” y=”9” width=”202” height=”437” fill=”url(#wallpaper)”/>
      <text x=”26” y=”53” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”13” font-weight=”700” fill=”white”>9:41</text>
      <rect x=”155” y=”47” width=”4” height=”8”  rx=”1” fill=”white”/>
      <rect x=”161” y=”44” width=”4” height=”11” rx=”1” fill=”white”/>
      <rect x=”167” y=”41” width=”4” height=”14” rx=”1” fill=”white”/>
      <rect x=”173” y=”38” width=”4” height=”17” rx=”1” fill=”white”/>
      <rect x=”182” y=”44” width=”22” height=”11” rx=”2.5” fill=”none” stroke=”white” stroke-width=”1.2” opacity=”0.8”/>
      <rect x=”204” y=”47” width=”3” height=”5” rx=”1” fill=”white” opacity=”0.8”/>
      <rect x=”183.5” y=”45.5” width=”16” height=”8” rx=”1.5” fill=”white” opacity=”0.8”/>
      <text x=”110” y=”108” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”46” font-weight=”200” fill=”white” text-anchor=”middle”>9:41</text>
      <text x=”110” y=”124” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”13” font-weight=”400” fill=”rgba(255,255,255,0.82)” text-anchor=”middle”>Monday, June 3</text>
      <rect x=”20” y=”162” width=”180” height=”74” rx=”16” fill=”rgba(242,242,247,0.92)” filter=”url(#cardShadow)”/>
      <rect x=”20” y=”162” width=”180” height=”74” rx=”16” fill=”none” stroke=”rgba(255,255,255,0.50)” stroke-width=”0.8”/>
      <!-- UPDATE 1: App icon background — change fill to client brand colour -->
      <rect x=”30” y=”172” width=”12” height=”12” rx=”3” fill=”#1B3F8B”/>
      <text x=”36” y=”181” font-family=”sans-serif” font-size=”7.5” font-weight=”800” fill=”white” text-anchor=”middle”>3</text>
      <!-- UPDATE 2: App name (all caps, short) -->
      <text x=”47” y=”181” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”9” font-weight=”500” fill=”#8E8E93”>CLIENT NAME</text>
      <text x=”192” y=”181” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”9” fill=”#AEAEB2” text-anchor=”end”>now</text>
      <!-- UPDATE 3: Notification title (bold, ~4 words) -->
      <text x=”30” y=”197” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”12” font-weight=”700” fill=”#1C1C1E”>Notification Headline</text>
      <!-- UPDATE 4: Body line 1 -->
      <text x=”30” y=”210” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”10.5” fill=”#3C3C43”>First line of notification body.</text>
      <!-- UPDATE 5: Body line 2 (~32 chars max) -->
      <text x=”30” y=”223” font-family=”-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,sans-serif” font-size=”10.5” fill=”#3C3C43”>Second line of notification body.</text>
      <rect x=”73” y=”438” width=”74” height=”5” rx=”2.5” fill=”white” opacity=”0.45”/>
    </g>
    <rect x=”70”  y=”16”  width=”80” height=”28” rx=”14” fill=”#111”/>
    <circle cx=”138” cy=”30” r=”7”   fill=”#0D0D0D”/>
    <circle cx=”138” cy=”30” r=”3.5” fill=”#181818”/>
    <circle cx=”136” cy=”28” r=”1.4” fill=”rgba(255,255,255,0.10)”/>
    <circle cx=”106” cy=”30” r=”2.5” fill=”#1A1A1A”/>
    <rect x=”-0.5” y=”82”  width=”5” height=”22” rx=”2.5” fill=”#252528”/>
    <rect x=”-0.5” y=”116” width=”5” height=”42” rx=”2.5” fill=”#252528”/>
    <rect x=”-0.5” y=”167” width=”5” height=”42” rx=”2.5” fill=”#252528”/>
    <rect x=”215.5” y=”130” width=”5” height=”58” rx=”2.5” fill=”#252528”/>
    <rect x=”73” y=”440” width=”74” height=”5” rx=”2.5” fill=”#666” opacity=”0.35”/>
    <rect x=”2” y=”2” width=”216” height=”451” rx=”46” fill=”none” stroke=”rgba(255,255,255,0.05)” stroke-width=”1”/>
  </svg>
</div>
```

**Phone wallpaper must always be near-black — never the brand colour.** Adapt the `wallpaper` gradient for each client using very dark tints only. For the Three Ireland template above, navy `#0a1a45` is dark enough. For a new client, derive very dark tints from their brand hex. Example for orange brand (`#FF6600`):

```html
<linearGradient id=”{PREFIX}-wallpaper” x1=”0.2” y1=”0” x2=”0.8” y2=”1”>
  <stop offset=”0%”   stop-color=”#1a0800”/>
  <stop offset=”50%”  stop-color=”#0f0500”/>
  <stop offset=”100%” stop-color=”#050200”/>
</linearGradient>
```

Using a bright stop (e.g. `stop-color=”#FF6600”`) makes the phone look like a branded rectangle with no screen. The lock screen clock and notification sit on top of this background — it must be dark enough for white text to be legible. Brand colour appears **only** in the notification app icon (`<rect x=”30” y=”172” width=”12” height=”12” ... fill=”[brandColour]”/>`) and CTA buttons — nowhere else on the phone.

**Notification text — count every character before writing.** At `font-size="10.5"`, the usable width inside the panel is ~170px (panel `x=20` width=180, text starts at `x=30`). At ~5.7px per character, this allows **≤29 characters per body line** safely. Count exactly — never estimate.

| Element | x position | Max chars |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| App name | `x=47` | 12 |
| Notification title (bold 12px) | `x=30` | 28 |
| Body line 1 (10.5px) | `x=30` | 29 |
| Body line 2 (10.5px) | `x=30` | 29 |

SVG `<text>` elements never wrap and have no overflow clipping by default — characters beyond the limit overflow the notification panel visibly. Keep it short and realistic — it should look like an actual push notification the customer receives as a result of the journey.

---

## Footer metrics

Three stats in the dark footer, pulled from client research:

```html
<div class="card-footer">
  <div class="metric-item">
    <div class="metric-value">5.4M</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Total Customers</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-item">
    <div class="metric-value">~50%</div>
    <div class="metric-label">Irish Mobile Market</div>
  </div>
  <div class="metric-item">
    <div class="metric-value">847K</div>
    <div class="metric-label">5G-Eligible Profiles</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

Stats must be real numbers from research â€” not invented. `.metric-value` is large bold white text; `.metric-label` is small uppercase muted text.

---

## Navigation

One button in the card header. It calls `showScreen()` â€” not an `href` link.

```html
<button class="nav-btn" onclick="showScreen('screen-journey')">View Journey â†’</button>
```

```css
#screen-card .nav-btn {
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--brand);
  background: none;
  border: none;
  cursor: pointer;
  opacity: 0.7;
  padding: 0;
}
#screen-card .nav-btn:hover { opacity: 1; }
```

---

## Asset dependency

The Adobe wordmark image is embedded as a base64 data URI directly in `three-use-case-card.html` â€” no separate PNG file needs to be referenced.

The product video **must be embedded as a base64 data URI** (see "Video embedding" above). No external video files should be referenced.
